curriculumData: 5. Amino acid and Protein chemistry and metabolism

qnaData.stem:
Study the clinical features of the case that is narrated below and answer the following questions related to the case:
A 58-year-old female with a 10-year history of severe chronic lower backache and morning stiffness visited the hospital OPD. She had no symptoms of nerve root compression but had a history of large joint pain in her hips, knees, and shoulders. A physical examination revealed bluish pigmentation over the forehead, cheeks, nose, fingertips, nails, hands, and ear cartilage. In addition, the sclera of both eyes showed dark brown pigment deposits, and there was dark brown pigmentation of the skin, teeth, and gums. When the patient's urine was left standing for a few hours at room temperature, it turned dark.
a) Diagnose the condition the patient is suffering from
b) Describe the metabolic pathway responsible for the above condition
c) Why does the patient have bluish/dark brown pigmentation over her body parts?
d) Name one confirmatory test to identify the condition [1+6+2+1=10]
qnaData.chapter: 5. Amino acid and Protein chemistry and metabolism

Study the clinical features of the case that is narrated below and answer the following questions related to the case:
A 30-year-old man comes to the clinic with complaints of constipation and severe abdominal pain. He is employed at battery assembly factory since the last two months. Since the past few days, he had developed weakness of his hands. He mentioned that he ate well, also he took vitamins along with iron supplements. There were no other neurological symptoms. Laboratory report revealed:
Haemoglobin - low; Serum iron and ferritin – high
a) What is the most probable diagnosis?
b) Which enzyme deficiency is responsible for this condition, describe the metabolic pathway?
c) What other test should be performed to confirm the diagnosis?
d) What is the cause of low haemoglobin levels? [2+4+2+2=10]

Study the clinical features of the case narrated below and answer the following questions:
A 20-year-old girl has developed yellowish discoloration of the skin. Her urine showed deep yellow color. Liver function test was done. The results showed total bilirubin is 5mg/dl and conjugated bilirubin is 2.3mg/dl, AST and ALT levels in serum were 800 U/L and 780 U/L respectively. (Normal reference range is < 40 U/L for both). ALP level did not show any significant elevation.
(a) What would be the most probable diagnosis?
(b) Explain the results of bilirubin in this case
(c) Comment on the levels of the enzymes and their significance
(d) In which type of jaundice ALP level increases significantly. [2+3+3+2=10]

Study the clinical features of the case narrated below and answer the following questions:
A 45-year old man presented with complaints of gain in weight within last four months. He developed rounded face and in the upper back there was fat deposits. His skin showed bruise marks. He had been tested to have fasting hyperglycemia and hyperlipidemia. His ACTH level was recorded to be low and urinary free cortisol level was very high.
(a) What is the likely diagnosis?
(b) Why there was hyperlipidemia and hyperglycemia?
(c) Comment on the ACTH and cortisol level recorded.
(d) What is the role of dexamethasone suppression test? [2+4+2+2=10]

A 40-year old woman who was newly diagnosed with type-2 diabetes mellitus was found to have hyperlipoproteinemia. As an adjunct to diet and exercise she was advised Atorvastatin medication.
a. What is the type of hyperlipoproteinemia seen in diabetes mellitus and the biochemical basis underlying such change?
b. What is the mechanism of action of Atorvastatin?
c. Why HDL/LDL ratio is important to monitor?
d. What is Lp(a) and the significance of estimating this parameter in this patient? [3+2+3+2=10]
Explain with figure the mechanism of secondary active transport. Describe two pathways where such transport occurs in our body. [3+2=5]
Explain with oxygen dissociation curves the suitability of hemoglobin and myoglobin to their physiological roles [2.5+2.5=5]
Describe the chemiosmotic hypothesis of ATP synthesis. Why is the amount of ATP generated different for NADH and FADH2? [3+2=5]
Explain the mechanism of fatty liver in alcoholics. What is lipotrophic factor? Cite an example. [3+1+1=5]
How is energy derived from anaerobic glycolysis? Explain the clinical significance of anaerobic glycolysis. [1+4=5]
Describe the urea cycle. Explain the clinical significance of urea formation. [3+2=5]

qnaData.stem:
24 yr old women complaints of acute abdominal pain for last 24 hrs. Her ultrasound and blood reports are normal except for hypoglycemia. She is being treated with hemin infusion.
a. What could be the probable diagnosis in this case and enzyme defect?
b. Which intermediate is expected to be excreted more in urine?
c. Explain how hemin infusion helps to treat this condition.
d. Does the hypoglycemia have any relevance in this case?
e. Why does the intake of barbiturates precipitate an attack of porphyria? [1+1+1+1+1=5]
Identify the enzyme deficiency for the following clinical picture: Low lactic acid in blood after exercise [1]
Identify the enzyme deficiency for the following clinical picture: Hemolytic anaemia after primaquine treatment [1]
Identify the enzyme deficiency for the following clinical picture: Presence of Homogentisic acid in urine [1]

Identify the associated vitamin deficiency: In OPD, 60-year-old chronic smoker and alcoholic man on examination revealed memory disorientation, stomatitis, glossitis, diarrhea, and exfoliative dermatitis with some vesicles on erythematous bases on photo exposed sites such as his hands. [1]
Identify the associated vitamin deficiency: In ophthalmic OPD, 40-year-old presents with progressive keratinization of the cornea. [1]
Identify the associated vitamin deficiency: A patient presented with pale skin, sunken eyes, sore gums, muscle pain, loose teeth, and corkscrew-shaped hair. [1]
Identify the associated vitamin deficiency: 50-year-old woman with H/O terminal ileum removal presented with extreme paleness, Hb: 9 g/dL and MCV is higher than normal. [1]
Identify the associated vitamin deficiency: A patient presented with hemorrhage, anemia and prolonged clotting time. [1]

A 30 yrs old patient reports with an intense thirst with a strong craving for ice water. He passes large amounts of urine (8-10 L per day). Urine glucose not elevated. Urine osmolality was very low, but after vasopressin injection, it improved.
a. Name the clinical condition he is suffering from.
b. How do you explain the improvement in urine osmolality with vasopressin injection?
c. Where is the defect in this condition? [2+2+1=5]

12 yrs. old boy presented with H/O 4-5 kg weight loss, increased thirst and urination. On examination he was drowsy and had kussmaul breathing. His random blood glucose was 450mg/dl. Samples were sent for arterial blood gas analysis (ABG) and urine analysis.
a. What could be the probable diagnosis and etiology for the same?
b. What do you intent to check in urine? Name the test for it.
c. What changes do you expect with pH and bicarbonate levels in ABG?
d. How do you explain kussmaul breathing in this case?
e. What will be the status of circulating free fatty acids and why? [2x5=10]

a. What would be the antidote and why?
b. Why methanol is toxic? [3+2=5]

a. Hyperthyroidism
b. Pyrexia

SAQ: Explain the immunological effector mechanism for the following
a. TB Pathogen by macrophages
b. Antibody dependent cell lysis
c. Anaphylactic reaction
d. Inactivated COVID vaccine
e. Incompatible Blood transfusion. [5x1=5]

A 21-year old student has been complaining of flu-like illness for the past three days with vomiting and abdominal tenderness in the right upper quadrant since the past 24 hours. Recently the child had eaten food from the street vendor. On examination, the student was found to have jaundice. Investigations revealed: T Bil (3.3mg/dL), D Bil (1.2mg/dL), ALT – 2543 IU/L, AST – 1407 IU/L, ALP -134 U/L, GGT– 67 IU/L. Bile pigments in urine was positive.
a. What is the most common cause of this disorder and give a probable diagnosis?
b. Which enzyme was altered maximally and why?
c. How will you differentiate this case from a case of alcoholic hepatitis?
d. Why was bile pigments positive in the urine?.
e. What do you mean by Jaundice? Which type of jaundice is found in this case and Why? [1+2+2+2+3=10]

SAQ: A 5-yr. old boy is exhibiting a tendency to bite his lips as well as chewing of this fingers to the point it bleeds. On examination joints were swollen and tender.
a. What will you look for in the blood work up?
b. Name the disease and enzyme deficiency associated with it.
c. Name a drug to treat this condition and its mechanism of action. [1+2+2=5]

SAQ: Explain Why
a. Methotrexate therapy can cause drug resistance in cancer
b. p53 mutation can cause cancer. [2.5x2=5]

SAQ: A 9-month-old infant is suffering from kernicterus due to severe nonhemolytic jaundice. Serum analysis showed an increased concentration of indirect-reacting bilirubin with no detection of conjugated bilirubin
a. Name the disorder and the associated enzyme deficiency.
b. Explain the pathology behind kernicterus.
c. How do you treat hyperbilirubinemia in this case? [2+2+1=5]

SAQ: 51-year-old female patient complaints of losing weight, yet is continuously hungry and eating more than usual. She also reports that she is experiencing irritability, insomnia, diarrhea, muscle weakness, heart palpitations, and intolerance to heat. You suspect Graves’ disease.
a. What will the picture of thyroid function test in this case?
b. Name a test to diagnose this condition.
c. Explain the cause of loss in weight and hunger with increased food intake. [2+1+2=5]

SAQ: A 35 yrs. old apparently healthy individual on medical examination for insurance was found to have hemoglobin electrophoresis report as follows: HbA1: 62%, HbS: 35%, HbF:1%, HbA2: 1%. He is not anaemic.
a. What is your probable diagnosis?
b. Name the defect and explain as how you will diagnose this condition using Restriction Fragment Length Polymorphism.
c. What is the probability that his child can develop the disease? [1+3+1=5]
SAQ: Compare and contrast the mechanism of action of insulin and thyroid hormones [5]

A 5-year-old child was brought to OPD with complaints of mental retardation, delayed developmental milestones, hyperactivity and seizures. Child had light coloured hair, skin and unpleasant mousy body odour. Serum Phenylalanine was 30mg/dl (normal- 1-2mg/dl). Ferric chloride test showed transient blue green coloured urine.
i. What is your probable diagnosis?
ii. Mention the enzyme defect and the reaction catalysed by it.
iii. Write the biochemical basis for the
a. Light coloured hair and skin
b. Mousy body odour
c. Neurological manifestation
iv. Describe the steps for synthesis of any two biologically important compounds derived from tyrosine. [1+2+3+4=10]

A 16-year-old tribal boy from Odisha, presented with sudden onset abdominal pain and cramping, accompanied by chest pain and fever. He has had repeated episodes of pain and swelling of the fingers of both his hands since childhood. On examination, he is pale and has jaundice. His hemoglobin is 6g%, Total bilirubin is 10mg% and direct bilirubin is 0.5mg%.
a. What is the patient likely to be suffering from? Justify.
b. What is the biochemical defect responsible for this clinical condition?
c. What further lab investigations are required to diagnose the condition? [3+3+4=10]

A 51-year-old, diabetic overweight man visits the emergency room with a symptom of "indigestion" of 5 days' duration. He has also had bouts of sweating, malaise, and headache. His blood pressure is 140/105 mm Hg. An electrocardiogram (ECG) revealed changes from one performed 6 months earlier. After 6 hours of the onset of pain his blood test showed total CK level to be 490 IU/L (Ref range: <200 IU/L), CK-MB was 65 IU/L (Ref range: <7 IU/L) and Troponin-T was 0.6 ng/ml ((Ref range: <0.1ng/ml).
a. What is the diagnosis? Justify your diagnosis.
b. Why does Diabetes Mellitus increase the risk of this disorder?
c. What is the biochemical basis of use of Aspirin in this condition?
d. What are isoenzymes? [2+2+3+2+1=10]

A 55-year-old lady presented with complaints of fatigue, memory loss, difficulty in maintaining balance. On examination she was found to have pallor and neurological deficits. She had low levels of serum vitamin B12.
a. What are the causes of vitamin B12 deficiency?
b. Explain the functions of vitamin B12 in the body. [2+3=5]

A 34-year-old labourer was admitted to the casualty in an unconscious state after consuming local made cheap liquor. His arterial blood gas analysis shows blood pH = 7.1, HCO3- = 13mEq/L and pCO2 was low and pO2 is raised. Anion gap was higher than normal. The patient sample sent to external referral lab showed presence of formate in blood.
a. Identify and explain the type of pH imbalance?
b. What is the cause of such condition?
c. What is anion gap and what is the normal level; why it is increased in this condition?
d. Which type of compensation may be expected in this case? [3+2+3+2=10]

A 45 year-old male patient presented to orthopaedic OPD with history of pain and swelling of great toe. He consumes alcohol and prefers non vegetarian diet. He gave history of occasional such pain particularly after a night of alcohol consumption. On examination the great toe is swollen, red and tender. His blood investigation revealed uric acid level 10mg/dL.
a. What is the probable diagnosis?
b. Explain the biochemical basis for the clinical symptoms
c. Explain how alcohol precipitates this condition?
d. What is the difference between primary and secondary hyperuricemia?
e. What is the reference range of uric acid?
f. What is the line of management of such clinical condition? [1+2+2+2+1+2=10]

a. Retinoblastoma protein prevents cancer
b. Telomerase activity may be therapeutic target for cancer [2.5+2.5=5]
SAQ: What is cDNA library? Describe its biomedical applications. [2+3=5]

A 16-year-old girl of a low-income family from a remote hilly area presented with a round swelling on the right side of the neck. Examination showed a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland with a firm consistency. The TFT values are as follows: TSH-35 uIU/ml (Normal 0.36-5.5 uIU/L); T4- 50 nmol/L (reference value, 70-150 nmol/L); T3-1 nmol/L (reference value, 0.9- 2.8 nmol/L); serum-free T4, 1 pg/ml; (reference value, 4.4-12 pg/ml), and normal free T3, 2.7 pg/ml; (reference value, 1.0-4.3 pg/ml). Serum thyroglobulin was 701.5 ng/ml; (reference value, <60 ng/ml).
a. Explain the possible diagnosis of the above condition?
b. Which element is needed for thyroid hormone synthesis?
c. What is free thyroid hormone and why it is important to measure? [2+1+2=5]

A 65-year-old man presents with complains of pain abdomen, weight loss and itching, yellow discoloration of skin and sclera. He has been passing high coloured urine and pale stools. His bilirubin values are 20mg%, direct bilirubin is 15mg%.
a. Which type of jaundice is he likely to be suffering from? Justify.
b. Comment on the bilirubin values.
c. Which enzymes are raised?
d. What is the reason for itching? [2+1+1+1=5]

On examination, the boy showed severe pallor, frontal bossing and significant hepato-splenomegaly. His parents informed that the child underwent blood transfusions few times before.

Name the vitamin that might be deficient in this case? Enlist some important dietary sources.
Explain the role of this vitamin in physiological condition. [1+2+2=5]

A 60- year- old man was brought to the OPD of AIIMS with chief complaints of nausea, loss of appetite, excessive vomiting and abdominal pain. On examination his blood pressure was 130/70 mm of Hg, pulse rate of 92/min, respiratory rate of 12/min. Abdominal USG showed gastric obstruction secondary to peptic ulcer.
ABG analysis showed: pH=7.6, PaCO2=85 mm of Hg, PaO2=43.5 mm of Hg, HCO3=82 mmol/L.
Other biochemical parameters: Serum urea 28mg/dl, Serum Sodium : 142 mEq/L, Serum Potassium: 2.6 mEq/L, Serum Chloride : 50 mEq/L
a. What is your provisional diagnosis?
b. Explain the biochemical basis of the disorder
c. Enumerate the compensatory mechanisms that will try to restore the pH of this patient
d. Define anion gap. Calculate the anion gap for this patient [2+2+3+3=10]

A 2-year-old boy was brought to the Pediatrics OPD with complaints of delayed milestones and inactive nature. On examination- his posterior fontanelle was patent, he had a protuberant tongue, hypotonic muscle and was mentally retarded.
Lab results for TFT were as follows with their normal levels in brackets: S.T3: 0.06 ng/ml (1.2-1.9 ng/ml), S.T4: 1.5 µg/dl (5-12 µg/dl), S.TSH :70 µIU /ml (0.5-5 µIU/ml)
a. What is the diagnosis? Justify your answer.
b. Is the mental retardation preventable? Give reason for your answer.
c. Name another condition causing delayed closure of fontanelles and its basis?
d. Name two inborn errors of metabolism causing mental retardation?
e. Name two methods of hormone estimation. [5x2=10]
SAQ: What is Western Blotting? Describe the principle and steps. Give an account of its applications. [1+2+2=5]
SAQ: What are the tests done for Glomerular Function? What do you understand by tubular function tests? What is e GFR? [2+2+1=5]
SAQ: Explain regulation of gene expression in prokaryotes with the help of the Lac Operon model. Enumerate the importance of epigenetic regulation of gene expression with suitable examples. [2+3=5]
SAQ: Mention any two abnormalities associated with purine metabolism along with the biochemical cause and clinical findings. [2+3=5]
SAQ: Give example of any two antibiotics and their mechanisms of action. [2.5+2.5=5]
SAQ: Explain the role of p53 in cancer. Name a genetic disorder leading to cancer due to DNA repair defect? [4+1=5]

A 5 day old neonate who was discharged on the 3rd day of life was readmitted with yellowish discoloration of the skin noticed by the mother. He was feeding well and his cry and activity was normal with no fever. The urine output was adequate and he was passing normal stools as well. O/E He looked well with normal activity and cry and had jaundice till the lower abdomen. The palms and soles were spared. Stools were golden yellow and urine was clear.
Her Lab reports are as follows: Total bilirubin- 10.5mg/dl, Direct bilirubin- 0.2 mg/dl
a. Identify the abnormal lab report(s).
b. What is the diagnosis?
c. What is the first line of treatment?
d. What is biochemical basis of this condition and treatment?
e. What is breast milk jaundice? [5x1=5]
SAQ: What is G-Protein and why it is called so. Enlist three hormones which acts through G Protein associated receptors? [2+3=5]
